message.content: nvm ignore the pgu then since its irrelevant. can u tell me, what is the most feature rich but most beautiful analytics servers i can run on my home server? like really simple to use https://rybbit.com/docs/self-hosting i like rybbit. how do other solutions compare?

content.text:
Good taste — Rybbit is genuinely one of the best-looking analytics tools right now. Here's how the realistic alternatives stack up.

### The serious contenders

| | Look & feel | Features | Infra weight | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Rybbit** (your pick) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Modern, dark-mode native, the prettiest | Real-time, sessions, funnels, retention, world map, custom events | Medium (Bun + ClickHouse + Postgres) | Privacy-first analytics with a 2025-era UI |
| **Umami** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Clean, minimal, mature polish | Pageviews, events, basic funnels, UTM, real-time | **Light** (Node + Postgres OR MySQL) | "Just give me Plausible but easier to self-host" |
| **PostHog** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dense but well-designed | **Massive**: analytics + session replay + feature flags + A/B + surveys + error tracking + LLM observability | **Heavy** (ClickHouse + Postgres + Kafka + Zookeeper + Redis + MinIO + 6 services) | Product analytics where you actually care about *behavior*, not just pageviews |
| **Plausible CE** | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Beautifully restrained | Pageviews, events, goals, basic funnels | Medium (Elixir + ClickHouse + Postgres) | Used to be the default; **avoid for new self-hosts** — they've been moving features to cloud-only since 2024 |
| **Matomo** | ⭐⭐ Functional but dated | Most features of any OSS analytics (it's the GA replacement) | Heavy (PHP + MySQL + lots of plugins) | If you need GA feature parity at any UX cost |
| **GoatCounter** | ⭐⭐⭐ Minimalist, charming | Pageviews only, no events | **Ultra light** (single Go binary + SQLite) | Personal blogs, "I just want pageview counts" |

### How they compare on the dimensions that matter

**Privacy / cookies**: All except PostHog and Matomo are cookieless by default. Rybbit, Umami, Plausible are GDPR-compliant out of the box. PostHog requires cookie consent if you enable session replay.

**Tracking script size** (page speed impact):
- GoatCounter: ~1 KB
- Plausible / Umami: ~1 KB
- Rybbit: ~3 KB
- PostHog: ~50 KB (the price of all those features)
- Matomo: ~22 KB

**Real-time**: Rybbit, PostHog, Umami all have real-time. Plausible CE removed real-time from self-host (cloud-only now — exact reason to avoid).

**Sessions vs unique visitors**: PostHog and Rybbit do real session reconstruction. Umami counts unique visitors but session details are shallow. Goat/Plausible CE: visitor counts only.

**Self-hosting story**:
- Easiest: **Umami** (single container + Postgres, one compose file, done)
- Easy: **Rybbit** (docker compose with Bun + ClickHouse, 2-3 services)
- Manageable: **PostHog** (offers a docker-compose for "small instances" but officially recommends Helm/k8s for prod)
- Pain: **Plausible CE** (Elixir release + ClickHouse + Postgres, manual migration steps)
- Pain: **Matomo** (PHP-FPM + nginx + MySQL + cron jobs)

### Honest recommendation for your setup

You already run langfuse (LLM tracing) + grafana stack (infra metrics) + cloudflared + beszel. So you've got infra observability covered.

What you're missing is **product analytics for heygaia.io and any sites you ship**. Two realistic picks:

1. **Stick with Rybbit** — your taste is right. It's the best-looking option, actively developed, and the feature set covers 90% of what you'd actually use. Self-host is well-documented. The ClickHouse dependency adds ~500MB RAM but you've got 47GB.

2. **Pick PostHog if you'll actually use feature flags + session replay** — otherwise it's massive overkill. Session replay alone is worth it if you're debugging UX issues on a product like heygaia.io. But running it adds ~3-4GB of RAM across its 6 services. Don't install it just because it has more features — only if you'll use them.

Skip the rest. Plausible CE is being hollowed out, Matomo is ugly, Umami is fine but Rybbit dominates it on aesthetics, GoatCounter is too minimal for your use case.

### Want me to install Rybbit?

Same pattern as everything else — rootless docker compose under gaia, tailscale serve at a port like `:7443`, point heygaia.io's analytics script at it. Should take ~10 min. I'd create a task and execute end-to-end. Say go and I'll do it.

# Rybbit Self-Hosting Extract

Based on the documentation provided, here's what I can confirm:

## 1. Docker Compose Setup
The page references a setup script (`setup.sh`) but **does not provide the actual Docker Compose YAML**. You're directed to either:
- Use the automatic setup script: `./setup.sh your.domain.name`
- Check the "[Manual Docker Compose Setup](/docs/self-hosting-guides/self-hosting-manual)" guide (not included in this content)

## 2. Required Services
Not explicitly listed. The page only mentions you'll need the setup script to configure environment variables.

## 3. Environment Variables
The documentation states: "The script will create a `.env` file" but **does not detail variable names or purposes**. Only mentioned:
- `--mapbox-token` (optional, for 3D maps)
- References to a "secure secret" being generated automatically

## 4. Default Port
**Not specified** in this excerpt.

## 5. Tracking Script Endpoint
**Not provided** in this quick-start guide. See the "[Tracking script](/docs/script)" documentation instead.

## 6. Adding Sites
**Not documented here.** The page only states: "Create your admin account. You can then log in and start adding your websites!" (no procedural detail).

## 7. Feature List
The navigation menu lists: autocapture, goals, funnels, bot detection, tagging, user identification. **Heatmaps, session replay, A/B testing, and feature flags are not mentioned.**

## 8. Hardware Requirements
"At least 2GB of RAM" recommended; ARM must be ARMv8.2-A or newer.

## 9. Backup Recommendations
**Not covered** in this excerpt.

## 10. CORS/Cross-Origin
**Not addressed** here.

## 11. Reverse-Proxy Notes
Caddy is auto-configured by the setup script. Advanced guides reference "[Manual Docker Compose Setup](/docs/self-hosting-guides/self-hosting-manual)" and "[Advanced Self-Hosting Guide](/docs/self-hosting-guides/self-hosting-advanced)" (not included).

## 12. Multi-Tenancy
**Not discussed** in this document.

---

**Recommendation:** You'll need to access the full Docker Compose file and manual setup guides linked but not provided here. The quick-start relies heavily on the automated `setup.sh` script, which abstracts away configuration details.

2. Required env vars in .env (with defaults and what they do)
3. Default web UI port
4. Path of the tracking script users embed
5. Whether sub-paths like /api/track or /api/script need to be reachable from the public (vs /admin which can be tailnet-only)

content.content:
I appreciate the detailed request, but I need to be transparent: **the webpage content provided doesn't include the actual `docker-compose.yml` file itself** — only instructions referencing it.

Here's what the documentation **does** contain:

## Available Information

**Required .env variables** (from the guide):
```
DOMAIN_NAME=your.domain.com
BASE_URL=https://your.domain.com
REDACTED
DISABLE_SIGNUP=false
REDACTED
HOST_BACKEND_PORT="3001:3001"
HOST_CLIENT_PORT="3002:3002"
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=frog
POSTGRES_USER=frog
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=frog
POSTGRES_DB=analytics
CLICKHOUSE_DB=analytics
```

**Services & Ports**:
- Client (Next.js frontend): port 3002
- Backend (Node.js API): port 3001
- PostgreSQL: database
- ClickHouse: analytics storage
- Caddy: optional reverse proxy

**Volumes** (stated): `rybbit_clickhouse-data`, `rybbit_postgres-data`

## What's Missing

The actual `docker-compose.yml` content isn't reproduced in this webpage. The guide says to "git clone https://github.com/rybbit-io/rybbit.git" to get it.

For your rootless Docker + Cloudflare Tunnel setup, you'd need to:
1. Clone the repo directly
2. Modify docker-compose.yml to remove Caddy
3. Expose ports 3001/3002

I cannot provide the verbatim YAML without access to the actual repository file.

input.command: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rybbit-io/rybbit/master/docker-compose.yml 2>&1 | head -200
input.description: Fetch actual Rybbit docker-compose.yml from GitHub

content.content:
services:
  caddy:
    profiles: ["with-webserver"]
    image: caddy:2.10.0
    container_name: caddy
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
      - "443:443/udp" # Needed for HTTP/3
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile # Mount Caddy config file
      - caddy_data:/data # Mount persistent data volume for certs etc.
      - caddy_config:/config # Mount persistent config volume
    environment:
      # Pass domain name for use in Caddyfile
      # Email is configured via Caddyfile global options
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME}
    depends_on:
      - backend
      - client

  clickhouse:
    container_name: clickhouse
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:25.4.2
    volumes:
      - clickhouse-data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    configs:
      - source: clickhouse_network
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/network.xml

... (141 more lines, 5229 bytes total)

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod

content.subject: Uninstall rootful Docker packages from home server
content.description: Daemon is already stopped+disabled (#15). Next step: apt purge docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras (keep rootless tooling that's needed). Remove /var/lib/docker, /var/lib/containerd. Verify rootless still works after. Defer until Portainer is verified working + backups are confirmed safe to discard.

content.subject: Enable GPU monitoring in Beszel for home server
content.description: Home server has a GPU (need to identify model — likely NVIDIA given the i7-10700K + workstation form). Beszel auto-detects GPUs if nvidia-smi (or AMD rocm-smi) is available inside the agent container. Steps: (1) identify GPU vendor + model via lspci on host, (2) ensure host has NVIDIA driver + nvidia-container-toolkit installed (or AMD equivalent), (3) configure rootless docker for GPU access (rootless NVIDIA setup is nontrivial — see nvidia-container-toolkit rootless mode docs), (4) update home beszel-agent compose to add runtime: nvidia + NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=all OR mount /dev/nvidia* devices, (5) restart agent, (6) verify GPU panel appears in Beszel dashboard with utilization/temp/memory.

content.subject: Move previews/ outside the staging-infra git tree (prevent future stash sweep)
content.description: Root cause of previews 403: previews/ dir lives inside /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra (a git working tree) and is untracked + not in .gitignore. git stash -u (and git clean -fdx, git checkout) will sweep it. Two-part fix: (1) add `previews/` to staging-infra/.gitignore so future stash/clean/checkout skip it, (2) better: change the bind mount source in the gaia-staging compose to a path OUTSIDE the git tree, e.g. /home/gaia/gaia-staging/.previewctl/runtime/previews-html/. Update previewctl's output dir config + the staging-infra compose volume binding. Verify by recreating + running a stash -u test.
content.activeForm: Moving previews dir outside git tree

file.content:
services:
  caddy:
    profiles: ["with-webserver"]
    image: caddy:2.10.0
    container_name: caddy
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
      - "443:443/udp" # Needed for HTTP/3
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile # Mount Caddy config file
      - caddy_data:/data # Mount persistent data volume for certs etc.
      - caddy_config:/config # Mount persistent config volume
    environment:
      # Pass domain name for use in Caddyfile
      # Email is configured via Caddyfile global options
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME}
    depends_on:
      - backend
      - client

  clickhouse:
    container_name: clickhouse
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:25.4.2
    volumes:
      - clickhouse-data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    configs:
      - source: clickhouse_network
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/network.xml
      - source: clickhouse_json
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/enable_json.xml
      - source: clickhouse_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logging_rules.xml
      - source: clickhouse_user_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/user_logging.xml
    environment:
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_USER=${CLICKHOUSE_USER:-default}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD:-frog}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8123/ping"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  postgres:
    image: postgres:17.4
    container_name: postgres
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-frog}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-frog}
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  backend:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-backend:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: backend
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: server/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "${HOST_BACKEND_PORT:-127.0.0.1:3001:3001}"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - CLICKHOUSE_HOST=http://clickhouse:8123
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD:-frog}
      - POSTGRES_HOST=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PORT=5432
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-frog}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-frog}
      - BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=${BETTER_AUTH_SECRET}
      - BASE_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
      - DISABLE_TELEMETRY=${DISABLE_TELEMETRY}
      - MAPBOX_TOKEN=${MAPBOX_TOKEN}
    depends_on:
      clickhouse:
        condition: service_healthy
      postgres:
        condition: service_started
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/health"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  client:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-client:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: client
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: client/Dockerfile
      args:
        NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL: ${BASE_URL}
        NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_SIGNUP: ${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
    ports:
      - "${HOST_CLIENT_PORT:-127.0.0.1:3002:3002}"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
    depends_on:
      - backend
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  clickhouse-data:
  postgres-data:
  caddy_data: # Persistent volume for Caddy's certificates and state
  caddy_config: # Persistent volume for Caddy's configuration cache (optional but good practice)

configs:
  clickhouse_network:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <listen_host>0.0.0.0</listen_host>
      </clickhouse>

  clickhouse_json:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <settings>
              <enable_json_type>1</enable_json_type>
          </settings>
      </clickhouse>

  clickhouse_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <logger>
            <level>warning</level>
            <console>true</console>
        </logger>
        <query_thread_log remove="remove"/>
        <query_log remove="remove"/>
        <text_log remove="remove"/>
        <trace_log remove="remove"/>
        <metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <session_log remove="remove"/>
        <part_log remove="remove"/>
        <latency_log remove="remove"/>
        <processors_profile_log remove="remove"/>
      </clickhouse>

  clickhouse_user_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <profiles>
          <default>
            <log_queries>0</log_queries>
            <log_query_threads>0</log_query_threads>
            <log_processors_profiles>0</log_processors_profiles>
          </default>
        </profiles>
      </clickhouse>

file.content:
# Domain and URL Configuration
DOMAIN_NAME=demo.rybbit.com
BASE_URL="https://${DOMAIN_NAME}"

# Authentication and Security
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=insecure-secret
DISABLE_SIGNUP=false

# Webserver Configuration
# Set to false to disable the built-in Caddy webserver
USE_WEBSERVER=true
# Host port mappings - these control how services are exposed
# Format: "host_binding:container_port" or "port:container_port"
HOST_BACKEND_PORT=127.0.0.1:3001
HOST_CLIENT_PORT=127.0.0.1:3002

# ClickHouse Database Configuration
CLICKHOUSE_DB=analytics
CLICKHOUSE_USER=default
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=frog

# PostgreSQL Database Configuration
POSTGRES_DB=analytics
POSTGRES_USER=frog
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=frog

# External Service Tokens (optional)
MAPBOX_TOKEN=

input.description: Deploy Rybbit (client + backend + clickhouse + postgres) as rootless docker compose at /home/gaia/rybbit/. No bundled caddy. Bind client:3002 + backend:3001 to 127.0.0.1. Add tailscale serve mapping at :7443 with path routing (/api -> backend, / -> client). Generate strong BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, postgres + clickhouse passwords. DISABLE_SIGNUP=true. BASE_URL initially https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443 (will swap to https://as.heygaia.io once cloudflared/DNS is in place).

content.text: Now installing Rybbit.

input.command:
ssh gaia-home-server bash <<'OUTER'
cat > /tmp/install_rybbit.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
gaia_docker() { sudo -u gaia env HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock "$@"; }
log() { echo; echo "============================================"; echo "=== $*"; echo "============================================"; }

log "PHASE A: dir + secrets"
sudo -u gaia mkdir -p /home/gaia/rybbit

BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
CH_PWD=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
PG_PWD=$(openssl rand -hex 16)

log "PHASE B: write compose + .env"
sudo -u gaia tee /home/gaia/rybbit/.env > /dev/null <<ENV
DOMAIN_NAME=gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
BASE_URL=https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=${BETTER_AUTH_SECRET}
DISABLE_SIGNUP=true
DISABLE_TELEMETRY=true
HOST_BACKEND_PORT=127.0.0.1:3010:3001
HOST_CLIENT_PORT=127.0.0.1:3011:3002
CLICKHOUSE_DB=analytics
CLICKHOUSE_USER=default
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CH_PWD}
POSTGRES_DB=analytics
POSTGRES_USER=rybbit
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${PG_PWD}
MAPBOX_TOKEN=
IMAGE_TAG=latest
ENV
chmod 600 /home/gaia/rybbit/.env
chown gaia:gaia /home/gaia/rybbit/.env

# Note: host ports remapped to 3010/3011 because 3000 is taken by langfuse-web
sudo -u gaia tee /home/gaia/rybbit/compose.yaml > /dev/null <<'YAML'
services:
  rybbit-clickhouse:
    container_name: rybbit-clickhouse
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:25.4.2
    volumes:
      - clickhouse-data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    configs:
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_network
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/network.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_json
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/enable_json.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logging_rules.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_user_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/user_logging.xml
    environment:
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_USER=${CLICKHOUSE_USER:-default}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8123/ping"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-postgres:
    image: postgres:17.4
    container_name: rybbit-postgres
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-rybbit}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-backend:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-backend:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: rybbit-backend
    ports:
      - "${HOST_BACKEND_PORT}"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - CLICKHOUSE_HOST=http://rybbit-clickhouse:8123
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_HOST=rybbit-postgres
      - POSTGRES_PORT=5432
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-rybbit}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=${BETTER_AUTH_SECRET}
      - BASE_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
      - DISABLE_TELEMETRY=${DISABLE_TELEMETRY}
      - MAPBOX_TOKEN=${MAPBOX_TOKEN}
    depends_on:
      rybbit-clickhouse:
        condition: service_healthy
      rybbit-postgres:
        condition: service_started
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/health"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-client:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-client:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: rybbit-client
    ports:
      - "${HOST_CLIENT_PORT}"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
    depends_on:
      - rybbit-backend
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  clickhouse-data:
  postgres-data:

configs:
  rybbit_clickhouse_network:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <listen_host>0.0.0.0</listen_host>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_json:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <settings>
              <enable_json_type>1</enable_json_type>
          </settings>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <logger>
            <level>warning</level>
            <console>true</console>
        </logger>
        <query_thread_log remove="remove"/>
        <query_log remove="remove"/>
        <text_log remove="remove"/>
        <trace_log remove="remove"/>
        <metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <session_log remove="remove"/>
        <part_log remove="remove"/>
        <latency_log remove="remove"/>
        <processors_profile_log remove="remove"/>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_user_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <profiles>
          <default>
            <log_queries>0</log_queries>
            <log_query_threads>0</log_query_threads>
            <log_processors_profiles>0</log_processors_profiles>
          </default>
        </profiles>
      </clickhouse>
YAML
chown gaia:gaia /home/gaia/rybbit/compose.yaml
echo "--- files ---"
ls -la /home/gaia/rybbit/
echo
echo "--- compose snippet ---"
head -25 /home/gaia/rybbit/compose.yaml
echo "..."

log "PHASE C: pull + start"
sudo -u gaia bash -c '
  export HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock
  cd /home/gaia/rybbit && docker compose up -d
'

log "PHASE D: wait + verify health"
sleep 30
gaia_docker docker ps --filter name=rybbit --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}"
echo
echo "--- backend health ---"
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  http://127.0.0.1:3010/api/health => HTTP %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:3010/api/health
echo "--- client root ---"
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  http://127.0.0.1:3011/ => HTTP %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:3011/

log "PHASE E: tailscale serve at :7443"
# / -> client, /api -> backend
tailscale serve --bg --https=7443 --set-path=/api http://127.0.0.1:3010 2>&1 | tail -5
tailscale serve --bg --https=7443 --set-path=/ http://127.0.0.1:3011 2>&1 | tail -5
echo
echo "--- updated serve config ---"
tailscale serve status 2>&1
echo
echo "--- end-to-end via tailnet ---"
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  https://...:7443/ => HTTP %{http_code}\n" https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443/
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  https://...:7443/api/health => HTTP %{http_code}\n" https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443/api/health

echo
echo "============================================"
echo "✓ Rybbit installed."
echo "  UI: https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443/"
echo "  Open it now to create admin user (DISABLE_SIGNUP=true means after the first user is created, no more signups)."
echo "============================================"
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/install_rybbit.sh
echo theexperiencecompany | sudo -S -p '' bash /tmp/install_rybbit.sh
OUTER
input.description: Install Rybbit end-to-end (rootless docker + tailscale serve)

content.text: All 4 containers up + healthy, UI responds 200, but `/api/health` returns 404 over tailscale serve — `--set-path` is stripping the `/api` prefix before forwarding. Cleanest fix: drop path-based tailscale routing and put a tiny caddy in front locally (same shape as Rybbit's own Caddyfile).
[Request interrupted by user]

content.text: Smart — using the existing `gaia-staging-traefik-1` instead of running yet another proxy. Let me figure out how it discovers services and what rules are configured.

input.command:
ssh gaia-home-server bash <<'OUTER'
cat > /tmp/inspect_traefik.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
export HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock

echo "=== traefik container inspect (cmd, networks, mounts) ==="
docker inspect gaia-staging-traefik-1 --format 'Cmd: {{.Config.Cmd}}
Entrypoint: {{.Config.Entrypoint}}
Networks: {{range $k,$v := .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{$k}} ({{$v.IPAddress}}) {{end}}
Mounts:'
docker inspect gaia-staging-traefik-1 --format '{{range .Mounts}}  {{.Type}} {{.Source}} -> {{.Destination}}{{println}}{{end}}'
echo
echo "=== traefik static config (mounted files) ==="
for f in $(docker inspect gaia-staging-traefik-1 --format '{{range .Mounts}}{{if eq .Type "bind"}}{{.Source}}{{println}}{{end}}{{end}}'); do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    echo "--- $f ---"
    cat "$f"
  elif [ -d "$f" ]; then
    echo "--- $f/ (dir) ---"
    ls -la "$f"
    for inner in "$f"/*.{yml,yaml,toml,json}; do
      if [ -f "$inner" ]; then echo "  --- $inner ---"; cat "$inner"; fi
    done 2>/dev/null
  fi
done
echo
echo "=== gaia-staging compose location ==="
find /home/gaia/gaia-staging /home/gaia/staging-infra -maxdepth 5 -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -l "traefik" 2>/dev/null | head -5
echo
echo "=== traefik api (catalog of routers) ==="
curl -sk http://127.0.0.1:18080/api/http/routers 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>&1 | head -80 || \
  curl -sk http://127.0.0.1:18080/api/http/routers 2>&1 | head -200
echo
echo "=== example service labels (one PR preview container) ==="
docker inspect gaia-pr-0-web --format '{{range $k,$v := .Config.Labels}}{{if (and (gt (len $k) 7) (eq (slice $k 0 7) "traefik"))}}  {{$k}}={{$v}}
{{end}}{{end}}'
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/inspect_traefik.sh
echo theexperiencecompany | sudo -S -p '' -u gaia /tmp/inspect_traefik.sh
OUTER

aiTitle: Set up Beszel on home server

content.text:
Got the layout. Traefik routes via **file provider** + **docker provider** (label-based), uses `gaia_core_net` network, has entrypoints `web` (:80→host:18081) + `pr8101..pr8120`. Cleanest integration: add a new entrypoint dedicated to Rybbit, route via file provider config, connect rybbit containers to `gaia_core_net`.

Let me find the compose definition for traefik so I can add the port.

input.command:
ssh gaia-home-server bash <<'OUTER'
cat > /tmp/find_traefik_compose.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== compose files in staging-infra ==="
ls -la /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/compose/
echo
echo "=== core.yml (traefik definition) ==="
grep -A 30 -B 1 'traefik:' /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/compose/core.yml
echo
echo "=== network gaia_core_net definition ==="
grep -A 5 'gaia_core_net\|networks:' /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/compose/core.yml | head -40
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/find_traefik_compose.sh
sudo -u gaia /tmp/find_traefik_compose.sh
OUTER

---

  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.1
    restart: unless-stopped
    command:
      - --configFile=/etc/traefik/traefik.yml
    ports:

content: or should we use the rybbit caddy configuration already given???

message.content: or should we use the rybbit caddy configuration already given???

input.command:
ssh gaia-home-server bash <<'OUTER'
cat > /tmp/rybbit_caddy.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
gaia_docker() { sudo -u gaia env HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock "$@"; }
log() { echo; echo "=== $*"; }

log "Phase A: remove old path-routing tailscale serve config for :7443"
tailscale serve --https=7443 off 2>&1 | tail -3 || true

log "Phase B: write Caddyfile + update compose to add rybbit-caddy"
sudo -u gaia tee /home/gaia/rybbit/Caddyfile > /dev/null <<'CADDY'
:80 {
  encode zstd gzip
  request_body max_size 10MB
  handle /api/* {
    reverse_proxy rybbit-backend:3001
  }
  handle {
    reverse_proxy rybbit-client:3002
  }
}
CADDY

# Append rybbit-caddy service to existing compose
sudo -u gaia tee /home/gaia/rybbit/compose.yaml > /dev/null <<'YAML'
services:
  rybbit-clickhouse:
    container_name: rybbit-clickhouse
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:25.4.2
    volumes:
      - clickhouse-data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    configs:
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_network
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/network.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_json
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/enable_json.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logging_rules.xml
      - source: rybbit_clickhouse_user_logging
        target: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/user_logging.xml
    environment:
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_USER=${CLICKHOUSE_USER:-default}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8123/ping"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-postgres:
    image: postgres:17.4
    container_name: rybbit-postgres
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-rybbit}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-backend:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-backend:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: rybbit-backend
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - CLICKHOUSE_HOST=http://rybbit-clickhouse:8123
      - CLICKHOUSE_DB=${CLICKHOUSE_DB:-analytics}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=${CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}
      - POSTGRES_HOST=rybbit-postgres
      - POSTGRES_PORT=5432
      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-analytics}
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-rybbit}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=${BETTER_AUTH_SECRET}
      - BASE_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
      - DISABLE_TELEMETRY=${DISABLE_TELEMETRY}
      - MAPBOX_TOKEN=${MAPBOX_TOKEN}
    depends_on:
      rybbit-clickhouse:
        condition: service_healthy
      rybbit-postgres:
        condition: service_started
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/health"]
      interval: 3s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-client:
    image: ghcr.io/rybbit-io/rybbit-client:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: rybbit-client
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=${BASE_URL}
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_SIGNUP=${DISABLE_SIGNUP}
    depends_on:
      - rybbit-backend
    restart: unless-stopped

  rybbit-caddy:
    image: caddy:2.10.0
    container_name: rybbit-caddy
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:18086:80"
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile:ro
      - caddy-data:/data
      - caddy-config:/config
    depends_on:
      - rybbit-backend
      - rybbit-client

volumes:
  clickhouse-data:
  postgres-data:
  caddy-data:
  caddy-config:

configs:
  rybbit_clickhouse_network:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <listen_host>0.0.0.0</listen_host>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_json:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
          <settings>
              <enable_json_type>1</enable_json_type>
          </settings>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <logger>
            <level>warning</level>
            <console>true</console>
        </logger>
        <query_thread_log remove="remove"/>
        <query_log remove="remove"/>
        <text_log remove="remove"/>
        <trace_log remove="remove"/>
        <metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/>
        <session_log remove="remove"/>
        <part_log remove="remove"/>
        <latency_log remove="remove"/>
        <processors_profile_log remove="remove"/>
      </clickhouse>
  rybbit_clickhouse_user_logging:
    content: |
      <clickhouse>
        <profiles>
          <default>
            <log_queries>0</log_queries>
            <log_query_threads>0</log_query_threads>
            <log_processors_profiles>0</log_processors_profiles>
          </default>
        </profiles>
      </clickhouse>
YAML

# Drop the host port bindings from backend/client (caddy proxies to them via internal docker network)
# (already removed from compose above)
chown gaia:gaia /home/gaia/rybbit/compose.yaml /home/gaia/rybbit/Caddyfile

# Also drop HOST_BACKEND_PORT and HOST_CLIENT_PORT from .env since we don't need them anymore
sudo -u gaia sed -i '/^HOST_BACKEND_PORT\|^HOST_CLIENT_PORT/d' /home/gaia/rybbit/.env

log "Phase C: recreate stack"
sudo -u gaia bash -c '
  export HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock
  cd /home/gaia/rybbit && docker compose up -d --remove-orphans
'

log "Phase D: tailscale serve :7443 -> caddy"
tailscale serve --bg --https=7443 http://127.0.0.1:18086 2>&1 | tail -5

log "Phase E: verify"
sleep 8
gaia_docker docker ps --filter name=rybbit --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}"
echo
echo "-- caddy direct --"
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  127.0.0.1:18086/ => HTTP %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:18086/
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  127.0.0.1:18086/api/health => HTTP %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:18086/api/health
echo "-- tailnet --"
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  https://...:7443/ => HTTP %{http_code}\n" https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443/
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  https://...:7443/api/health => HTTP %{http_code}\n" https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443/api/health
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/rybbit_caddy.sh
echo theexperiencecompany | sudo -S -p '' bash /tmp/rybbit_caddy.sh
OUTER
input.description: Switch Rybbit to its bundled Caddy reverse proxy

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod

content.subject: Uninstall rootful Docker packages from home server
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Daemon is already stopped+disabled (#15). Next step: apt purge docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras. Remove /var/lib/docker, /var/lib/containerd. Verify rootless still works after.

content.subject: Move previews/ outside the staging-infra git tree (prevent future stash sweep)
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Root cause: previews dir lives in /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra (a git working tree), untracked + not gitignored. git stash -u swept it. Fix: (1) add `previews/` to .gitignore, (2) move bind mount source outside repo, e.g. /home/gaia/gaia-staging/.previewctl/runtime/previews-html/.
content.activeForm: Moving previews dir outside git tree

content.subject: Install Rybbit on home server (rootless docker, tailnet-only first)
content.description: Deploy Rybbit (client + backend + clickhouse + postgres) as rootless docker compose at /home/gaia/rybbit/. No bundled caddy. Bind client:3002 + backend:3001 to 127.0.0.1. Add tailscale serve mapping at :7443 with path routing (/api -> backend, / -> client). Generate strong BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, postgres + clickhouse passwords. DISABLE_SIGNUP=true. BASE_URL initially https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443 (will swap to https://as.heygaia.io once cloudflared/DNS is in place).

content.subject: Set up public DNS + TLS for as.heygaia.io -> home Rybbit
content.description: heygaia.io DNS is on Porkbun; cloudflared currently only routes jellyfin.aryanranderiya.com (which is on Cloudflare DNS). Need to either: (a) migrate heygaia.io DNS to Cloudflare to use existing tunnel, (b) add Tailscale Funnel for public exposure with CNAME from Porkbun, or (c) keep heygaia.io on Porkbun + use Cloudflare Workers as a relay. Once decided, add ingress: as.heygaia.io/api/* -> http://localhost:3001 and as.heygaia.io/* -> http://localhost:3002. Then update Rybbit BASE_URL.
content.activeForm: Setting up public DNS for as.heygaia.io

content.description: After admin user created in Rybbit UI, add 5 sites: staging-test (for verification), aryanranderiya.com, heygaia.io, ui.heygaia.io, motion.heygaia.io. Collect site IDs (small integer) + tracking snippets for each.

content.subject: Verify every Rybbit feature works in staging environment
content.description: Inject Rybbit tracking script into one of the gaia-staging PR preview web containers (e.g. pr-0 which serves the develop branch heygaia.io). Browse the staging URL on tailnet, click around, scroll, navigate routes, trigger custom events. Verify in Rybbit dashboard: pageviews, sessions, unique users, real-time, world map, referrers, UTM, device/browser/OS detection, screen resolution, custom events, goals/conversions, funnels, user identification, bot detection, autocapture (if Rybbit has it), retention cohorts. Document any feature that doesn't work cleanly. Only after 100% pass: proceed to production PRs.

content.text: Oh — my mistake. I set `DISABLE_SIGNUP=true` thinking it would only block *future* signups after the first admin. But it blocks **all** signups including the first one. Fixing now — temporarily enabling signup so you can create the admin, then locking it down again.

grep DISABLE_SIGNUP .env
echo
export HOME=/home/gaia XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock
echo "--- recreating backend + client (DISABLE_SIGNUP is baked-in for client at build, but server checks runtime too) ---"
docker compose up -d --force-recreate rybbit-backend rybbit-client 2>&1 | tail -10
sleep 5
echo
echo "--- backend logs ---"
docker logs rybbit-backend --tail 10 2>&1
INNER
OUTER

aiTitle: Set up Beszel on home server

content.text: heygaia.io is **fully on Porkbun** (with subdomains pointing at Vercel, Hetzner, Better Stack, etc.). aryanranderiya.com is on Cloudflare which is why the jellyfin tunnel works there. So for `as.heygaia.io` we have two realistic paths:

questions.question: How should we route as.heygaia.io publicly to the home server?

options.label: Caddy on gaia-prod proxies as.heygaia.io → home over tailnet
options.description: Keep heygaia.io DNS on Porkbun. Add an A-record `as.heygaia.io → 178.104.125.59` (your gaia-prod public IP). Install Caddy on gaia-prod to terminate TLS (auto Let's Encrypt) and reverse-proxy to home rybbit via tailnet. NO other heygaia.io services touched. I can do this end-to-end. ~10 min.

options.label: Migrate heygaia.io DNS from Porkbun to Cloudflare
options.description: Then add a CF Tunnel ingress. Cleanest long-term and unlocks CF edge features (DDoS, caching, fast DNS), but you have to do the CF account / nameserver change at Porkbun yourself. I guide you through it. ~15 min including DNS propagation.

options.label: Use as.aryanranderiya.com instead (CF already)
options.description: Compromise on the URL. Add a CF DNS record + tunnel ingress. Fastest (~3 min) but not the URL you wanted.

 how to migrate to cloudflare? theres no export button from porlbun but cloudflare has button for bind zone file.

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod

content.subject: Uninstall rootful Docker packages from home server
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Daemon is already stopped+disabled (#15). Next step: apt purge docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras. Remove /var/lib/docker, /var/lib/containerd. Verify rootless still works after.

content.subject: Move previews/ outside the staging-infra git tree (prevent future stash sweep)
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Root cause: previews dir lives in /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra (a git working tree), untracked + not gitignored. git stash -u swept it. Fix: (1) add `previews/` to .gitignore, (2) move bind mount source outside repo, e.g. /home/gaia/gaia-staging/.previewctl/runtime/previews-html/.
content.activeForm: Moving previews dir outside git tree

content.subject: Install Rybbit on home server (rootless docker, tailnet-only first)
content.description: Deploy Rybbit (client + backend + clickhouse + postgres) as rootless docker compose at /home/gaia/rybbit/. No bundled caddy. Bind client:3002 + backend:3001 to 127.0.0.1. Add tailscale serve mapping at :7443 with path routing (/api -> backend, / -> client). Generate strong BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, postgres + clickhouse passwords. DISABLE_SIGNUP=true. BASE_URL initially https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443 (will swap to https://as.heygaia.io once cloudflared/DNS is in place).

content.subject: Set up public DNS + TLS for as.heygaia.io -> home Rybbit
content.description: heygaia.io DNS is on Porkbun; cloudflared currently only routes jellyfin.aryanranderiya.com (which is on Cloudflare DNS). Need to either: (a) migrate heygaia.io DNS to Cloudflare to use existing tunnel, (b) add Tailscale Funnel for public exposure with CNAME from Porkbun, or (c) keep heygaia.io on Porkbun + use Cloudflare Workers as a relay. Once decided, add ingress: as.heygaia.io/api/* -> http://localhost:3001 and as.heygaia.io/* -> http://localhost:3002. Then update Rybbit BASE_URL.
content.activeForm: Setting up public DNS for as.heygaia.io

content.subject: Create Rybbit sites: staging + aryanranderiya.com + heygaia.io + ui.heygaia.io + motion.heygaia.io
content.description: After admin user created in Rybbit UI, add 5 sites: staging-test (for verification), aryanranderiya.com, heygaia.io, ui.heygaia.io, motion.heygaia.io. Collect site IDs (small integer) + tracking snippets for each.

content.subject: Verify every Rybbit feature works in staging environment
content.description: Inject Rybbit tracking script into one of the gaia-staging PR preview web containers (e.g. pr-0 which serves the develop branch heygaia.io). Browse the staging URL on tailnet, click around, scroll, navigate routes, trigger custom events. Verify in Rybbit dashboard: pageviews, sessions, unique users, real-time, world map, referrers, UTM, device/browser/OS detection, screen resolution, custom events, goals/conversions, funnels, user identification, bot detection, autocapture (if Rybbit has it), retention cohorts. Document any feature that doesn't work cleanly. Only after 100% pass: proceed to production PRs.

content.subject: PR: add Rybbit tracking to aryanranderiya.com (portfolio)
content.description: Local repo at /Users/aryan/Projects/Web/portfolio. Add Rybbit script with site ID from #25 to the Next.js app (likely Script tag in app/layout.tsx or _document.tsx). Branch + PR.

content.subject: PR: add Rybbit tracking to heygaia.io (apps/web in gaia monorepo)
content.description: Local repo at /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-develop/apps/web (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19). Add Rybbit Script in src/app/layout.tsx (root) or appropriate locale layout. Branch + PR against develop.

aiTitle: Set up Beszel on home server

content.text:
Yep — easiest is to build a BIND zone file from what you pasted, then use Cloudflare's **Import DNS Records** feature. I've converted all 37 records below.

### Step-by-step

1. **Cloudflare → Add a Site** → enter `heygaia.io` → pick **Free** plan
2. Cloudflare will auto-scan and pre-populate some records. **Skip/clear that** and use Import instead:
3. On the DNS records page → top-right **"Import and Export"** → **Import DNS Records** → upload the file below (save as `heygaia.io.zone`)
4. After import, review records — most should be **gray cloud (DNS only)**. Only flip to orange/proxied for things that genuinely benefit (we'll proxy the new `as.heygaia.io` later). For anything pointing at Vercel/Hetzner/external SaaS, keep **DNS only** so they handle their own TLS.
5. Cloudflare gives you **2 nameservers** at the bottom of the Overview page (random assignment like `xena.ns.cloudflare.com`).
6. Porkbun → click **Edit Authoritative Nameservers** (the link you saw) → replace the 4 Porkbun nameservers with the **2 from Cloudflare**.
7. Wait. Usually 5-30 min for CF to detect the change. CF will email you when site is **Active**.
8. Once Active, ping me and I'll add the tunnel ingress for `as.heygaia.io`.

### The BIND zone file

```bind
$ORIGIN heygaia.io.
$TTL 600

; ----- A records -----
@                                                IN A     216.150.1.1
api                                              IN A     178.104.125.59
logs                                             IN A     178.104.125.59

; ----- ALIAS (treated as CNAME — none are at apex) -----
whatsapp                                         IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
blog                                             IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
discord                                          IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
staging                                          IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.

; ----- CNAME records -----
www                                              IN CNAME dbfa3bf5c2ef0192.vercel-dns-016.com.
ui                                               IN CNAME 5c9b65a6a6e674b0.vercel-dns-017.com.
docs                                             IN CNAME cname.vercel-dns.com.
motion                                           IN CNAME 7f6d9858fe01d1ff.vercel-dns-017.com.
icons                                            IN CNAME ce366aeb1d0366e7.vercel-dns-017.com.
status                                           IN CNAME statuspage.betteruptime.com.
roadmap                                          IN CNAME customdomains.featurebase.app.
experience                                       IN CNAME experience-dng.pages.dev.
z                                                IN CNAME 211707a092b0632cb759.cf-prod-us-proxy.proxyhog.com.
b6sgx6gwpnz5                                     IN CNAME gv-vt7grrwkozh4w4.dv.googlehosted.com.
*.discord                                        IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
*.blog                                           IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
*.staging                                        IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.
*.whatsapp                                       IN CNAME uixie.porkbun.com.

; ----- MX -----
@                                                IN MX 1  smtp.google.com.
send                                             IN MX 10 feedback-smtp.ap-northeast-1.amazonses.com.

; ----- TXT -----
@                                                IN TXT   "v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all"
@                                                IN TXT   "REDACTED"
send                                             IN TXT   "v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all"
resend._domainkey                                IN TXT   "REDACTED/SU//REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED"
_acme-challenge                                  IN TXT   "REDACTED"
_acme-challenge                                  IN TXT   "REDACTED"
_discord                                         IN TXT   "dh=7578061e6774ee6197913fa4e16d892a2dd986ac"
_dmarc                                           IN TXT   "v=DMARC1; p=none;"
_gh-hey-gaia-o                                   IN TXT   "28a0cf1d05"
_github-pages-challenge-theexperiencecompany.ui  IN TXT   "41ab2e0c39a7e8560d2c7ccb9fa6e2"
_vercel                                          IN TXT   "vc-domain-verify=docs.heygaia.io,d53592d4c7c666f53f77"
_vercel                                          IN TXT   "vc-domain-verify=heygaia.io,439c73c4e6e9e976ec59"
_vercel                                          IN TXT   "vc-domain-verify=heygaia.io,5c3ad2ca9a369e90425d"
_vercel                                          IN TXT   "vc-domain-verify=www.heygaia.io,7408a4d394d82d5924a2"
```

### Things to know

| Issue | What to do |
|---|---|
| **DNSSEC** | Porkbun shows you have 1 DNSSEC record. **Disable DNSSEC at Porkbun BEFORE switching nameservers** or your domain breaks (DS record won't match CF's keys). Re-enable in Cloudflare later (Cloudflare → DNS → Settings → DNSSEC). |
| **Apex A record going to Vercel** | After import, **keep gray cloud (DNS only)**. Vercel handles TLS for its IP. If proxied, you'll get cert errors. |
| **api.heygaia.io → 178.104.125.59** | Same — keep **DNS only**. Direct connection to your prod box. |
| **Email (MX + SPF)** | All MX/SPF/DKIM/DMARC records are TXT/MX — these are always DNS-only (you can't proxy email). |
| **TTL drop trick** | Not needed here — switching nameservers is a separate signal from per-record TTL. Just expect ~30 min for full propagation. |
| **Email outage during migration** | Possible 5-15 min gap if a sender is mid-lookup at the moment NS switches. Most retry, so impact is minimal. |

### Updated task

input.description: User chose Cloudflare migration. STEPS: (1) Add heygaia.io to user's CF account, (2) Import the BIND zone file I prepared (37 records), (3) Disable DNSSEC at Porkbun first to avoid breakage, (4) Switch NS at Porkbun to CF's 2 assigned nameservers, (5) Wait for CF to mark site Active (~30 min), (6) Add cloudflared ingress on home server: as.heygaia.io -> http://localhost:18086 (rybbit-caddy), (7) Update DNS proxied/orange-cloud for as.heygaia.io only, (8) Update Rybbit BASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL to https://as.heygaia.io, recreate rybbit-backend + rybbit-client, (9) Verify https://as.heygaia.io loads from public internet, /api/health returns 200.

message.content: great they're uploaded. what all should be proxied what shouldnt be??

content.text:
For your current 37 records: **all of them should be DNS-only (gray cloud) for now.** None benefit from Cloudflare proxy because they all point to external SaaS that handles its own TLS. Proxying any of them would either break things or add a useless hop.

Here's the rundown:

### Everything should be gray cloud (DNS only)

| Record | Why DNS-only |
|---|---|
| `@ → 216.150.1.1` (Vercel) | Vercel's IP handles TLS for your apex |
| `api → 178.104.125.59` (Hetzner) | Your prod box does its own TLS; proxying could break WebSockets/SSE |
| `logs → 178.104.125.59` | Same — your prod box |
| `www → vercel-dns-016.com` | Vercel handles TLS |
| `ui → vercel-dns-017.com` | Vercel handles TLS |
| `docs → vercel-dns.com` | Vercel handles TLS |
| `motion → vercel-dns-017.com` | Vercel handles TLS |
| `icons → vercel-dns-017.com` | Vercel handles TLS |
| `status → betteruptime.com` | Better Stack manages TLS for your custom status page |
| `roadmap → featurebase.app` | Featurebase manages TLS |
| `experience → pages.dev` | Cloudflare Pages has its own custom-domain attachment — proxying through a different zone breaks it |
| `z → proxyhog.com` | Third-party proxy already, don't double-proxy |
| `b6sgx6gwpnz5 → googlehosted.com` | Google domain verification — must be unproxied |
| `whatsapp / blog / discord / staging → uixie.porkbun.com` | Porkbun URL forwarding doesn't work behind a proxy |
| All wildcards (`*.discord`, `*.blog`, etc.) | Same as their parents |
| MX records (smtp.google.com, amazonses) | Cloudflare can't proxy email at all |
| All TXT records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, verifications) | Cloudflare can't proxy TXT |

### What about the upcoming as.heygaia.io?

**That one WILL be orange (proxied)** — it's mandatory for Cloudflare Tunnel to work. But I'll add that record + flip the cloud for you once Cloudflare shows your zone is "Active". You don't need to add it manually right now.

### What to do now

1. Scan your DNS records list in Cloudflare. Anything that came in as **orange (proxied)** by default → click it → toggle to gray (Proxy status: DNS only) → Save.
2. Confirm all records are gray.
3. Continue with the nameserver switch at Porkbun.

(Heads-up: CF often defaults A and CNAME records to orange on import — usually 5-10 of yours need flipping.)

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: (Heads-up: CF often defaults A and CNAME records to orange on import — usually 5-10 of yours need flipping.) why do they? should they not be proxied? is this not the best approach?

content.text:
Fair pushback — the answer's actually nuanced. Two reasons CF defaults to proxied:

1. **Their business model.** Proxied = CF sees the traffic = they can offer caching, WAF, DDoS, analytics, Workers, Access etc. Non-proxied = they're just a nameserver. Their pitch ("the best CDN") only kicks in when proxied. So defaults to "on."
2. **It works for the typical user.** Most people on CF Free are running a single WordPress/static site on a VPS they own. For that pattern, proxying is genuinely a free upgrade — free TLS, free DDoS, hides origin IP, edge caching.

### Why it's NOT right for your records specifically

Your case is unusual: **almost everything points to managed SaaS that has its own edge network.** Stacking CF in front of those breaks the model:

| Origin | What proxying does |
|---|---|
| **Vercel** (www, ui, docs, motion, icons, @) | Adds an extra hop in front of Vercel's already-global edge → slower, not faster. Plus their domain attachment is verified via their CNAME — CF proxy can interfere. Vercel's docs explicitly say "don't proxy through CF unless you really know why." |
| **Better Stack, Featurebase, Pages.dev** | These are managed status/community/Pages services with custom domain integration. Proxying through CF means their TLS handshake target changes — usually breaks. |
| **Porkbun URL forwarding** (`uixie.porkbun.com`) | The forwarder issues an HTTP redirect; if CF proxies, the redirect target comes from CF IPs which Porkbun's filtering will reject. |
| **DNS verification records** (`_acme-challenge`, `_domainkey`, `_vercel`, etc.) | Must be DNS-only for resolution. Proxying these breaks the verifying party. |

### The one debatable case: `api → 178.104.125.59` (your Hetzner box)

This is your own infra, so proxying is *technically possible*. Trade-offs:

**Proxy it (orange) if you want:**
- Free DDoS / bot protection on the API
- Hide the Hetzner IP from script kiddies
- CF's WAF rules

**Don't proxy (gray) if any of this matters:**
- API uses WebSockets / SSE / long-polling (works but needs extra CF config; gray = no friction)
- You need true client IP without parsing `CF-Connecting-IP`
- API requests can exceed 100s (CF Free terminates at 100s — long-running endpoints break)
- Specific clients have IP allowlists for your origin (CF rotates IPs)
- Faster debugging — direct connection, no CF in the middle

For GAIA's API (LLM agent, probably has long-running calls + maybe websockets for chat streaming), I'd lean **gray** — keep it direct. You can flip to orange later if you actually want CF's protection and have time to handle the friction.

### The principle

> Proxy what YOU host on a server YOU control. Leave alone anything pointing at a third-party SaaS.

By that rule for your zone: **all 37 stay gray**. Then the new `as.heygaia.io` (which we're about to add, pointing at YOUR home server via CF Tunnel) goes orange — perfect fit.

CF's default isn't wrong, it's just optimized for a different use case than yours.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: so heygaia.io will be on cloudflare pages. should i proxy www. and normal??

content.text:
If they're **actually moving to Cloudflare Pages**, then YES — proxy both. But there's a timing thing:

### Order matters

| Current state | Proxy setting |
|---|---|
| Still pointing at Vercel (`@ → 216.150.1.1`, `www → vercel-dns-016.com`) | **Gray (DNS-only)**. Don't proxy a Vercel origin — adds latency, fights with Vercel's edge. |
| Migrated to CF Pages (`@` + `www` → your `<project>.pages.dev`) | **Orange (proxied)** — required for CF Pages custom domains to work |

If you flip to orange *before* changing the record target away from Vercel, you'll get cert errors and broken pages.

### The right way to attach a custom domain to a CF Pages project

Skip the manual DNS edit entirely — let Pages do it for you:

1. CF Dashboard → **Workers & Pages** → your `heygaia` project (or whatever it's named)
2. **Custom domains** tab → **Set up a custom domain**
3. Enter `heygaia.io` → CF will:
   - Detect the existing A record pointing to Vercel
   - Offer to replace it with a CNAME to the Pages project
   - Auto-set it to proxied (orange)
   - Auto-issue a cert
4. Repeat for `www.heygaia.io`

This is cleaner than editing the DNS records manually — CF wires up everything correctly in one click.

### What about right now?

Two scenarios:

**(A) You're moving to Pages later** → leave `@` and `www` records pointing at Vercel, gray cloud, no changes. When you actually deploy to Pages, do the custom-domain flow above.

**(B) You're already deploying to Pages and want to cut over now** → use the custom-domain flow above for both `@` and `www`. CF handles the DNS + proxy state automatically.

Which scenario are you in? If (B), the Pages cutover and the Rybbit tunnel setup are independent and can both happen in parallel.

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod

content.subject: Uninstall rootful Docker packages from home server
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Daemon is already stopped+disabled (#15). Next step: apt purge docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras. Remove /var/lib/docker, /var/lib/containerd. Verify rootless still works after.

content.subject: Move previews/ outside the staging-infra git tree (prevent future stash sweep)
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Root cause: previews dir lives in /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra (a git working tree), untracked + not gitignored. git stash -u swept it. Fix: (1) add `previews/` to .gitignore, (2) move bind mount source outside repo, e.g. /home/gaia/gaia-staging/.previewctl/runtime/previews-html/.
content.activeForm: Moving previews dir outside git tree

content.subject: Install Rybbit on home server (rootless docker, tailnet-only first)
content.description: Deploy Rybbit (client + backend + clickhouse + postgres) as rootless docker compose at /home/gaia/rybbit/. No bundled caddy. Bind client:3002 + backend:3001 to 127.0.0.1. Add tailscale serve mapping at :7443 with path routing (/api -> backend, / -> client). Generate strong BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, postgres + clickhouse passwords. DISABLE_SIGNUP=true. BASE_URL initially https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443 (will swap to https://as.heygaia.io once cloudflared/DNS is in place).

content.subject: Set up public DNS + TLS for as.heygaia.io -> home Rybbit
content.description: User chose Cloudflare migration. STEPS: (1) Add heygaia.io to user's CF account, (2) Import the BIND zone file I prepared (37 records), (3) Disable DNSSEC at Porkbun first to avoid breakage, (4) Switch NS at Porkbun to CF's 2 assigned nameservers, (5) Wait for CF to mark site Active (~30 min), (6) Add cloudflared ingress on home server: as.heygaia.io -> http://localhost:18086 (rybbit-caddy), (7) Update DNS proxied/orange-cloud for as.heygaia.io only, (8) Update Rybbit BASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL to https://as.heygaia.io, recreate rybbit-backend + rybbit-client, (9) Verify https://as.heygaia.io loads from public internet, /api/health returns 200.

content.text:
Hold on — **the migration hasn't actually taken effect yet.** DNS still shows Porkbun's nameservers:

```
maceio.ns.porkbun.com.
curitiba.ns.porkbun.com.
salvador.ns.porkbun.com.
fortaleza.ns.porkbun.com.
```

Did you change the nameservers at Porkbun yet, or just add the zone in Cloudflare? Two possibilities:

**(A) Forgot to update NS at Porkbun** — Cloudflare has the zone set up but the world still asks Porkbun for records. Go to **Porkbun → heygaia.io → Edit Authoritative Nameservers** → replace all 4 Porkbun ones with the 2 Cloudflare gave you.

**(B) NS updated, still propagating** — usually 5-30 min, sometimes up to a few hours. Run `dig +short NS heygaia.io @1.1.1.1` periodically; once you see `*.ns.cloudflare.com`, you're good.

Actually you DID switch. **Whois confirms** the registrar updated to `arvind.ns.cloudflare.com` + `clarissa.ns.cloudflare.com`. But public resolvers (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8) still have the old Porkbun delegation cached — `.io` TLD's delegation TTLs are several hours.

#!/bin/bash
set -e

log() { echo; echo "=== $*"; }

log "PHASE B: update config.yml to add as.heygaia.io ingress"
cat > /home/gaia/.cloudflared/config.yml <<'YAML'
tunnel: 05ee3c5a-c569-430c-91cc-e1e046e1d956

  - hostname: jellyfin.aryanranderiya.com
    service: http://localhost:8096
  - hostname: as.heygaia.io
    service: http://localhost:18086
  - service: http_status:404
YAML
cat /home/gaia/.cloudflared/config.yml

log "PHASE C: create DNS route via cloudflared CLI inside container"
docker exec cloudflared cloudflared tunnel route dns 05ee3c5a-c569-430c-91cc-e1e046e1d956 as.heygaia.io 2>&1

log "PHASE D: restart cloudflared to pick up new ingress"
docker restart cloudflared 2>&1
sleep 8
docker logs cloudflared --tail 15 2>&1 | tail -15

log "PHASE E: update Rybbit BASE_URL to https://as.heygaia.io"

sed -i 's|^DOMAIN_NAME=.*|DOMAIN_NAME=as.heygaia.io|' .env

log "PHASE F: recreate rybbit-backend + rybbit-client to pick up new BASE_URL"

sleep 8

log "PHASE G: verify"
docker ps --filter name=rybbit --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}"
echo

curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "  https://as.heygaia.io/api/health => HTTP %{http_code}\n" -m 10 https://as.heygaia.io/api/health
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/setup_as_tunnel.sh

content.text:
Hit a snag — **the cloudflared CLI created the DNS record in the wrong zone**:

```
Added CNAME as.heygaia.io.aryanranderiya.com which will route to this tunnel
```

It made `as.heygaia.io.aryanranderiya.com` (a subdomain in your `aryanranderiya.com` zone) instead of `as.heygaia.io` in the `heygaia.io` zone. Why: the cloudflared cert (`cert.pem`) you authorized once back in May was scoped to `aryanranderiya.com`. When you added `heygaia.io` to your CF account, that cert didn't auto-extend permissions to it.

### Quick fix — two manual clicks in Cloudflare

**Clean up the wrong record:**
1. CF dashboard → **aryanranderiya.com** zone → DNS → find the entry `as.heygaia.io` (CNAME → `cfargotunnel.com`) → **Delete**

**Add the correct record:**
2. CF dashboard → **heygaia.io** zone → DNS → **Add record**:
   - Type: **CNAME**
   - Name: `as`
   - Target: `05ee3c5a-c569-430c-91cc-e1e046e1d956.cfargotunnel.com`
   - Proxy status: **Proxied** (orange cloud)
   - TTL: Auto
   - **Save**

That's it. The cloudflared ingress on the home server is already configured for `as.heygaia.io` → home Rybbit. Once the DNS record exists in heygaia.io zone, the tunnel routes traffic correctly.

Ping me once you've made the swap and I'll verify https://as.heygaia.io is reachable + finalize the Rybbit BASE_URL setup.

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod

content.subject: Uninstall rootful Docker packages from home server
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Daemon is already stopped+disabled (#15). Next step: apt purge docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras. Remove /var/lib/docker, /var/lib/containerd. Verify rootless still works after.

content.subject: Move previews/ outside the staging-infra git tree (prevent future stash sweep)
content.description: USER-GATED. Do NOT start until user explicitly says go. Root cause: previews dir lives in /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra (a git working tree), untracked + not gitignored. git stash -u swept it. Fix: (1) add `previews/` to .gitignore, (2) move bind mount source outside repo, e.g. /home/gaia/gaia-staging/.previewctl/runtime/previews-html/.
content.activeForm: Moving previews dir outside git tree

content.subject: Install Rybbit on home server (rootless docker, tailnet-only first)
content.description: Deploy Rybbit (client + backend + clickhouse + postgres) as rootless docker compose at /home/gaia/rybbit/. No bundled caddy. Bind client:3002 + backend:3001 to 127.0.0.1. Add tailscale serve mapping at :7443 with path routing (/api -> backend, / -> client). Generate strong BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, postgres + clickhouse passwords. DISABLE_SIGNUP=true. BASE_URL initially https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:7443 (will swap to https://as.heygaia.io once cloudflared/DNS is in place).

attachment.prompt: for some reason it shows pointing at jellyfin btw in dns records

content.text:
Got it — "points at jellyfin" tells me what's wrong. The CNAME target you entered is probably wrong.

DNS resolves to CF's anycast IPs (`104.21.3.26`, `172.67.130.19`) — that's correct for a proxied record. But HTTPS returns **HTTP 000** (TLS handshake fails entirely), which usually means CF Tunnel can't find a matching ingress rule for the hostname.

The CNAME target must be **exactly**:

```
05ee3c5a-c569-430c-91cc-e1e046e1d956.cfargotunnel.com
```

If you put `jellyfin.aryanranderiya.com` (or any other "human" hostname), CF would route it as if it were jellyfin, which doesn't have an ingress rule for `as.heygaia.io` → fails.

content.subject: Install Portainer CE on home server (rootless docker, tailscale-serve URL)
content.description: Install Portainer CE under gaia's rootless docker. Compose at /home/gaia/portainer/, mount the rootless docker socket so Portainer sees all 30 rootless containers. Expose via tailscale serve (separate port or path prefix). Create admin user. Verify dashboard shows containers + stacks. Edge agent on prod is tracked separately.

content.subject: Debug /previews 403 on gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net
content.description: ROOT CAUSE: stale bind mount inode. Container started 2026-05-26 17:42 when host dir /home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra/previews didn't yet exist (or was a different inode). Sometime later (host dir Birth: 2026-05-27 19:01), the dir was rm+recreated — kernel bind mount in container was still pinned to the old (deleted) inode. previewctl was happily writing index.html to the NEW inode every 60s, but nginx in the container only saw the OLD (empty) inode. FIX: docker restart gaia-staging-previews-1 — re-resolves the bind to the current inode. RECURRENCE RISK: anything that rm's + recreates the previews dir on the host will trigger this again. Long-term: have previewctl never rm the dir (only overwrite files), or use a named volume.

content.subject: Install Portainer Edge Agent on gaia-prod, register with home Portainer
content.description: Edge agent deployed as global swarm service `portainer_edge_agent` on gaia-prod. Two fixes were needed beyond the boilerplate Portainer-generated command: (1) added --host-add gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:100.126.190.120 to bypass Docker overlay DNS which doesn't know Tailscale MagicDNS; (2) updated home Portainer compose to also expose port 8000 (the Edge tunnel port) on the tailnet, not just 9000 (UI). After both fixes + a service --force restart, agent established websocket tunnel with ~160ms latency. gaia-prod now shows as second environment in home Portainer UI with full Swarm visibility (services, stacks, tasks).
content.activeForm: Installing Portainer Edge agent on prod
